questions.question: Which term describes a person who is overly self-centered?
questions.explanation: B) Narcissist (noun) – A person who is overly self-centered and excessively concerned with themselves आत्ममुग्ध/अत्यधिक आत्मकेन्द्रित व्यक्ति • Altruist (noun) – A person who shows selfless concern for others परोपकारी • Philanthropist (noun) – A person who donates money or help for the welfare of others दानवीर • Optimist (noun) – A person who tends to expect positive outcomes आशावादी

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences to form a meaningful and grammatically correct paragraph: P. He fuelled his body for the rigorous workout by adhering to a particular diet and drinking plenty of water. Q. He pushed his body to its limits by going on lengthy runs on the weekends. R. Throughout the week, he increased his endurance by running shorter distances. S. He was tired but elated when he crossed the finish line on race day
questions.explanation: B) P, R, Q, S He fuelled his body for the rigorous workout by adhering to a particular diet and drinking plenty of water. Throughout the week, he increased his endurance by running shorter distances He pushed his body to its limits by going on lengthy runs on the weekends. He was tired but elated when he crossed the finish line on race day Sentence P सबसे पहले आता है क्योंकि यह training की preparation stage introduce करता है। यहाँ “He fuelled his body” phrase बताता है कि intense workouts से पहले वह diet और hydration पर ध्यान देता है। यह passage की शुरुआत के लिए सही है क्योंकि physical effort से पहले body preparation logically आती है। Sentence R, Sentence P के बाद आता है क्योंकि अब actual weekly training routine explain किया जा रहा है। “Throughout the week” time marker दिखाता है और बताता है कि वह shorter distances run करके endurance बढ़ाता है। यह preparation के बाद regular practice को logically follow करता है। Sentence Q, Sentence R के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह training का intense phase दिखाता है। Weekend long runs body को limits तक push करने को दर्शाते हैं, जो weekly practice के next level के रूप में naturally fit होता है। Sentence S paragraph का conclusion है क्योंकि यह पूरे training process का final outcome बताता है। “Race day” completion stage है, और “tired but elated” emotions training के result को express करते हैं। इसलिए यह sentence अंत में आता है। Sentence P comes first because it introduces the preparation stage of the training. The phrase “He fuelled his body” shows that before starting intense physical activity, he focused on diet and hydration. Beginning with preparation creates a logical starting point for the paragraph. Sentence R follows Sentence P because it explains the regular weekly routine. The time expression “Throughout the week” clearly shows consistency, and running shorter distances helps build endurance. This naturally comes after body preparation. Sentence Q comes next as it describes the most demanding part of the training. Long weekend runs represent pushing the body to its limits, which logically follows the weekday endurance-building routine. Sentence S comes last because it shows the final result of all the training. “Race day” marks the completion of the entire process, and the emotions “tired but elated” reflect the outcome of sustained effort.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: She gave him a small wave before leaving.
options: The crowd gave a cheerful wave to the players.
A massive wave crashed onto the shore.
She gave him a friendly wave as she walked out the door.
He gave a final wave to his family as the plane took off.
questions.explanation: B) Highlighted वाक्य में ‘wave’ का अर्थ है — हाथ हिलाकर अभिवादन करना। विकल्प B में ‘wave’ का अर्थ है — समुद्र की लहर। उच्चारण और वर्तनी समान, पर अर्थ अलग — इसलिए यही homonym है। In the highlighted sentence, ‘wave’ means a hand gesture of greeting. In option B, ‘wave’ means a sea wave. Same spelling and pronunciation but different meanings — so B is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Reasonable.”
questions.explanation: B) Reasonable (adjective) – Based on or in accordance with fairness, logic, or sound judgment; moderate in price. (उचित/तर्कसंगत) Antonym: Preposterous (adjective) – Contrary to reason or common sense; utterly absurd or ridiculous in nature. (निरर्थक/बेतुका) • Philanthropic (adjective): Seeking to promote the welfare of others, typically by donating money to good causes. (परोपकारी) • Perspicacious (adjective): Having a ready insight into and understanding of things; mentally perceptive. (कुशाग्रबुद्धि) • Procrastinate (verb): To delay or postpone action; to put off doing something despite knowing the negative consequences. (टालमटोल करना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Rub.”
questions.explanation: D) Rub (noun) – A central problem or difficulty; an obstacle, hitch, snag, or problem in a situation. (समस्या/अड़चन) Antonym: Facilitation (noun) – The action of making a process or action easy or easier; the removal of obstacles. (सरलीकरण/सुविधा) • Fulmination (noun): An expression of very strong reproach or protest; an explosion. (गर्जना) • Fulguration (noun): The act of flashing like lightning; or the destruction of tissue. (चमक) • Fumigation (noun): The action of disinfecting or purifying an area with fumes. (धूमीकरण)

questions.question: Choose the option that has an error in the sentence. A bigger budget has been set aside for this years marketing campaign in Asia.
questions.explanation: C) for this years 'this years' के बदले 'this year’s' होगा क्योंकि यहाँ possessive form चाहिए, न कि plural; अभियान इस साल का है, कई सालों का नहीं; जैसे— this year’s plan. • 'this year’s' will be used instead of 'this years' because we need a possessive noun to show ownership, and the correct form is year + ’s.

questions.question: Choose the sentence with correct usage of "less" and "fewer".
options: She has less books than I do.
There are fewer sugar in the jar.
We need fewer volunteers this time.
He has fewer money than his brother.
questions.explanation: C) We need fewer volunteers this time. 'fewer' का प्रयोग countable plural nouns के साथ किया जाता है और volunteers एक countable plural noun है; इसलिए यह वाक्य सही है। • 'fewer volunteers' is correct because fewer is used with countable plural nouns, whereas less is used with uncountable nouns.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Virulent.”
questions.explanation: C) Virulent (adjective) – Extremely severe or harmful in its effects; bitterly hostile or lethal. (घातक/जहरीला) Synonym: Malignant (adjective) – Very virulent or infectious; tending to invade normal tissue or cause great harm. (हानिकारक/घातक) • Malleable (adjective): Easily influenced or controlled; (of metal) able to be hammered or pressed into shape. (लचीला) • Mandatory (adjective): Required by law or mandate; compulsory rather than optional. (अनिवार्य) • Monologue (noun): A long speech by one actor in a play or movie, or by one person in a conversation. (एकालाप)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate MEANING of the idiom “Feel the pinch.”
options: To pine, perish, or plummet financially
To procrastinate, prolong, or protract fatigue
To pontificate, preach, or prattle foolishly
To pillage, plunder, or pilfer frequently
questions.explanation: A) Feel the pinch (idiom) – To have problems because you have less money than you used to have. (तंगी महसूस करना)

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In today's technology-centric era, electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life. From smartphones and laptops to home appliances, the demand for newer and faster gadgets continues to escalate. However, this increasing dependence has brought about a critical challenge: electronic waste, commonly known as e-waste. E-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic equipment that is obsolete or non-functional. Unlike regular waste, it contains hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium. Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies, causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks. Developing nations are particularly vulnerable, as they often receive large volumes of e-waste from wealthier countries. In many cases, informal recycling sectors handle this waste without adequate safety measures. Workers, including children, dismantle devices manually, exposing themselves to toxic chemicals and dangerous working conditions. On the brighter side, responsible recycling of e-waste enables the recovery of valuable materials like copper, gold, and high-quality plastics. This not only mitigates environmental harm but also reduces the strain on natural resources. Governments and environmental agencies are advocating for sustainable practices such as repairing old devices, donating functional electronics, and following safe recycling methods. Public awareness, coupled with stringent regulations, is vital for tackling this escalating problem. In conclusion, managing e-waste effectively demands both individual accountability and collective efforts. Adopting eco-friendly habits can lead us toward a cleaner, safer, and more sustainable future. Identify the type of the following sentence and justify your answer: "Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies, causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks."
questions.explanation: A) ‘Simple sentence’ होगा क्योंकि इस वाक्य में केवल एक ही independent clause है — “Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies”। इसके बाद आने वाले “causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks” present participial phrases हैं, जो अतिरिक्त जानकारी देते हैं, न कि कोई नया clause जोड़ते हैं। इसलिए यह वाक्य simple sentence की श्रेणी में आता है। • ‘Simple sentence’ should be the correct answer because the sentence contains only one independent clause — “Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies.” The phrases “causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks” are participial phrases, which merely modify the main clause and do not form separate clauses. Hence, the sentence is a simple sentence.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In today's technology-centric era, electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life. From smartphones and laptops to home appliances, the demand for newer and faster gadgets continues to escalate. However, this increasing dependence has brought about a critical challenge: electronic waste, commonly known as e-waste. E-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic equipment that is obsolete or non-functional. Unlike regular waste, it contains hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium. Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies, causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks. Developing nations are particularly vulnerable, as they often receive large volumes of e-waste from wealthier countries. In many cases, informal recycling sectors handle this waste without adequate safety measures. Workers, including children, dismantle devices manually, exposing themselves to toxic chemicals and dangerous working conditions. On the brighter side, responsible recycling of e-waste enables the recovery of valuable materials like copper, gold, and high-quality plastics. This not only mitigates environmental harm but also reduces the strain on natural resources. Governments and environmental agencies are advocating for sustainable practices such as repairing old devices, donating functional electronics, and following safe recycling methods. Public awareness, coupled with stringent regulations, is vital for tackling this escalating problem. In conclusion, managing e-waste effectively demands both individual accountability and collective efforts. Adopting eco-friendly habits can lead us toward a cleaner, safer, and more sustainable future. Transform the above assertive sentence into an interrogative sentence. "Electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life."
options: Why electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life?
Had electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life?
Are electronic devices become indispensable to modern life?
Have electronic devices become indispensable to modern life?
questions.explanation: D) Have electronic devices become indispensable to modern life? Assertive से Interrogative Sentence में बदलते समय Helping Verb को Subject से पहले लाया जाता है और Main Verb का रूप वही रहता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "Electronic devices" Subject है, "have become" Verb (Present Perfect) है और "indispensable to modern life" Predicate है। Interrogative बनाते समय "have" को Subject से पहले रखा गया है। To change an assertive sentence into an interrogative sentence, the auxiliary (helping) verb is placed before the subject, while keeping the main verb unchanged and maintaining the same tense. Here, "Electronic devices" is the subject and "have become" is the verb in the Present Perfect tense. Moving have before the subject correctly forms the interrogative sentence.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In today's technology-centric era, electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life. From smartphones and laptops to home appliances, the demand for newer and faster gadgets continues to escalate. However, this increasing dependence has brought about a critical challenge: electronic waste, commonly known as e-waste. E-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic equipment that is obsolete or non-functional. Unlike regular waste, it contains hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium. Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies, causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks. Developing nations are particularly vulnerable, as they often receive large volumes of e-waste from wealthier countries. In many cases, informal recycling sectors handle this waste without adequate safety measures. Workers, including children, dismantle devices manually, exposing themselves to toxic chemicals and dangerous working conditions. On the brighter side, responsible recycling of e-waste enables the recovery of valuable materials like copper, gold, and high-quality plastics. This not only mitigates environmental harm but also reduces the strain on natural resources. Governments and environmental agencies are advocating for sustainable practices such as repairing old devices, donating functional electronics, and following safe recycling methods. Public awareness, coupled with stringent regulations, is vital for tackling this escalating problem. In conclusion, managing e-waste effectively demands both individual accountability and collective efforts. Adopting eco-friendly habits can lead us toward a cleaner, safer, and more sustainable future. What is the most appropriate meaning of the word "obsolete" as used in the sentence below? "E-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic equipment that is obsolete or non-functional."
options: Outdated and no longer in use
questions.explanation: C) ‘Outdated and no longer in use’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि वाक्य में obsolete उन उपकरणों के लिए प्रयुक्त हुआ है जो तकनीकी रूप से पुराने हो चुके हैं और अब प्रचलन या उपयोग में नहीं हैं। यहाँ obsolete or non-functional का अर्थ है कि उपकरण या तो पुराने पड़ चुके हैं या काम करने योग्य नहीं रहे। इसलिए obsolete का सबसे उपयुक्त अर्थ outdated and no longer in use होगा। • ‘Outdated and no longer in use’ should be used because in the given sentence, obsolete refers to devices that have become old due to technological advancement and are no longer used. The phrase obsolete or non-functional clearly indicates items that are outdated or unusable. Hence, outdated and no longer in use is the most appropriate meaning.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In today's technology-centric era, electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life. From smartphones and laptops to home appliances, the demand for newer and faster gadgets continues to escalate. However, this increasing dependence has brought about a critical challenge: electronic waste, commonly known as e-waste. E-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic equipment that is obsolete or non-functional. Unlike regular waste, it contains hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium. Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies, causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks. Developing nations are particularly vulnerable, as they often receive large volumes of e-waste from wealthier countries. In many cases, informal recycling sectors handle this waste without adequate safety measures. Workers, including children, dismantle devices manually, exposing themselves to toxic chemicals and dangerous working conditions. On the brighter side, responsible recycling of e-waste enables the recovery of valuable materials like copper, gold, and high-quality plastics. This not only mitigates environmental harm but also reduces the strain on natural resources. Governments and environmental agencies are advocating for sustainable practices such as repairing old devices, donating functional electronics, and following safe recycling methods. Public awareness, coupled with stringent regulations, is vital for tackling this escalating problem. In conclusion, managing e-waste effectively demands both individual accountability and collective efforts. Adopting eco-friendly habits can lead us toward a cleaner, safer, and more sustainable future. Which word is closest in meaning to "mitigate" as used in this sentence? "This not only mitigates environmental harm but also reduces the strain on natural resources."
questions.explanation: C) Mitigate (verb) – To make less severe, reduce, alleviate, diminish. कम करना Synonym: Lessens (verb) – Reduces, decreases, diminishes, alleviates. घटाना • Increases (verb) – Raises, enlarges, intensifies, amplifies. बढ़ाना • Explains (verb) – Clarifies, describes, interprets, elucidates. समझाना • Delays (verb) – Postpones, defers, puts off, stalls. विलंब करना

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In today's technology-centric era, electronic devices have become indispensable to modern life. From smartphones and laptops to home appliances, the demand for newer and faster gadgets continues to escalate. However, this increasing dependence has brought about a critical challenge: electronic waste, commonly known as e-waste. E-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic equipment that is obsolete or non-functional. Unlike regular waste, it contains hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium. Improper disposal of e-waste allows these toxins to seep into soil and water bodies, causing severe environmental degradation and posing long-term health risks. Developing nations are particularly vulnerable, as they often receive large volumes of e-waste from wealthier countries. In many cases, informal recycling sectors handle this waste without adequate safety measures. Workers, including children, dismantle devices manually, exposing themselves to toxic chemicals and dangerous working conditions. On the brighter side, responsible recycling of e-waste enables the recovery of valuable materials like copper, gold, and high-quality plastics. This not only mitigates environmental harm but also reduces the strain on natural resources. Governments and environmental agencies are advocating for sustainable practices such as repairing old devices, donating functional electronics, and following safe recycling methods. Public awareness, coupled with stringent regulations, is vital for tackling this escalating problem. In conclusion, managing e-waste effectively demands both individual accountability and collective efforts. Adopting eco-friendly habits can lead us toward a cleaner, safer, and more sustainable future. Why are developing countries more vulnerable to the dangers of e-waste, according to the passage?
options: They produce the most e-waste in the world
They often receive large volumes of e-waste and lack proper safety measures.
Their citizens prefer using outdated electronic devices.
questions.explanation: C) ‘They often receive large volumes of e-waste and lack proper safety measures’ सही उत्तर है क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि developing nations को wealthier countries से बड़ी मात्रा में e-waste मिलता है और उसे अक्सर informal recycling sectors द्वारा बिना उचित सुरक्षा उपायों के संभाला जाता है। इसी कारण workers, including children, toxic chemicals के संपर्क में आ जाते हैं, जिससे ये देश अधिक vulnerable हो जाते हैं। • ‘They often receive large volumes of e-waste and lack proper safety measures’ is correct because the passage clearly states that developing countries receive huge amounts of e-waste from richer nations and process it through informal recycling systems without adequate safety protections, increasing health and environmental risks.

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitution for the phrase in italics. "A person who is indifferent to or distrustful of human integrity or morality."
questions.explanation: B) Cynic (noun) – A person who is indifferent to or distrustful of human integrity or morality निंदक/संशयवादी • Idealist (noun) – A person guided by ideals rather than practical considerations आदर्शवादी • Optimist (noun) – A person who expects the best outcomes आशावादी • Humanitarian (noun) – A person concerned with promoting human welfare मानवतावादी

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Meddle.”
questions.explanation: A) Meddle (verb) – To interfere in or busy oneself unduly with something that is not one's concern. (हस्तक्षेप करना) Synonym: Interpose (verb) – To place or insert between one thing and another; to intervene or interfere. (हस्तक्षेप करना/बीच में आना) • Inundate (verb): To overwhelm with things; to flood. (अभिभूत करना) • Imprecate (verb): To utter a curse or invoke evil against someone. (शाप देना) • Indict (verb): To formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime. (अभियोग लगाना)

questions.question: Choose the option that is the most appropriate conversion of the following sentence into Active Voice. The mystery had never been solved by anyone before the detective arrived.
options: No one never solved the mystery before the detective arrived.
No one had solved the mystery before the detective arrived.
No one solves the mystery before the detective arrived.
No one has solved the mystery before the detective arrived.
questions.explanation: B) No one had solved the mystery before the detective arrived. Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Agent (by anyone) Active वाक्य का Subject बनता है और Verb को उसी Tense में Active रूप में बदला जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में Tense Past Perfect है। Passive वाक्य में “The mystery” Subject है, “had never been solved” Verb phrase है और “by anyone” Agent है। Active में बदलने पर Agent “No one” Subject बनता है और Verb had solved (Past Perfect Active) में बदला जाता है, जबकि “before the detective arrived” clause वही रहता है। Double negative से बचने के लिए “never” को हटा दिया जाता है। To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the agent of the passive sentence becomes the subject of the active sentence, and the verb is converted into its active form while keeping the same tense. Here, the sentence is in the Past Perfect tense. “The mystery” is the subject, “had never been solved” is the passive verb phrase, and “by anyone” is the agent. In the active form, “No one” becomes the subject and the verb changes to “had solved,” avoiding double negation.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate option: The CEO's sudden resignation sent ___ through the corporate world.
questions.explanation: A) ‘Tremors’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यह एक स्थापित मुहावरेदार अभिव्यक्ति है, जिसका अर्थ है किसी घटना का व्यापक और गंभीर प्रभाव पड़ना। यहाँ CEO के अचानक इस्तीफ़े ने पूरे corporate world में चिंता, अस्थिरता और प्रभाव की लहरें पैदा कीं, जिसे “sent tremors through” से सही ढंग से व्यक्त किया जाता है। इसलिए, “tremors” सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है। • ‘Tremors’ should be used because it is an idiomatic expression indicating a widespread and serious impact. The CEO’s sudden resignation caused shock and instability across the corporate world, which is accurately conveyed by the phrase “sent tremors through.” Thus, “tremors” is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The wedding ring was passed down through generations.
options: She wore a diamond ring on her left hand.
He proposed with a beautiful engagement ring.
Police busted a smuggling ring in the area.
The golden ring sparkled under the lights.
questions.explanation: C) Highlighted वाक्य में ‘ring’ का अर्थ है — उंगली में पहना जाने वाला आभूषण। विकल्प C में ‘ring’ का अर्थ है — अपराधियों का संगठित गिरोह (a criminal gang or network)। उच्चारण और वर्तनी समान हैं, लेकिन अर्थ अलग — इसलिए यह homonym का सही उदाहरण है। In the highlighted sentence, ‘ring’ refers to a piece of jewelry worn on the finger. In option C, ‘ring’ means an organized group involved in illegal activity. Same spelling and pronunciation but different meanings — hence option C is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate MEANING of the phrase “On a tailspin.”
options: To obfuscate, confuse, or blur a situation
To ostracize, exclude, or banish a member
To deteriorate, collapse, or spiral out of control
To obliterate, destroy, or wipe out a trace
questions.explanation: C) On a tailspin (phrase) – A situation in which something quickly becomes much worse in a way that is difficult to control. (नियंत्रण से बाहर होना)

questions.question: From the alternatives given below, select the correct passive voice of the following sentence. The strong current of the river broke through the wall of the earthen dam within no time.
options: The wall of the earthen dam was broken within no time by the strong current of the river.
The wall of the earthen dam was broken through within no time by the strong current of the river.
The wall of the earthen dam had broken through within no time by the strong current of the river.
The wall of the earthen dam had been broken through within no time by the strong current of the river.
questions.explanation: B) The wall of the earthen dam was broken through within no time by the strong current of the river. Active से Passive Voice में बदलने के लिए, Active वाक्य का Object Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Verb को उसी Tense के Passive रूप में बदला जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में Tense Simple Past है। Active वाक्य में “the strong current of the river” Subject है, “broke through” Verb phrase है और “the wall of the earthen dam” Object है। Passive में Object “the wall of the earthen dam” Subject बन जाता है और Verb phrase को was broken through (Simple Past Passive) में बदला जाता है, साथ ही Agent “by the strong current of the river” जोड़ा जाता है। To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence, and the verb is converted into its passive form while maintaining the same tense. Here, the sentence is in the Simple Past tense. “The strong current of the river” is the subject, “broke through” is the verb phrase, and “the wall of the earthen dam” is the object. In the passive construction, the correct form is “was broken through,” followed by the agent phrase.

questions.question: Choose the best option to replace the underlined part to improve the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select 'No substitution'. She has been working in this organisation since 5 years.
questions.explanation: B) for 'since 5 years' के बदले 'for 5 years' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि since का प्रयोग point of time के लिए होता है, जबकि for का प्रयोग period of time के लिए किया जाता है; जैसे— She has lived here for five years. • 'for 5 years' will be used instead of 'since 5 years' because for correctly denotes a duration of time, whereas since refers to a starting point.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate preposition: The hikers finally emerged ___ the dense forest just before sunset.
questions.explanation: B) ‘Out of’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि वाक्य में hikers के घने जंगल से बाहर निकलने की क्रिया बताई गई है। यहाँ emerged क्रिया किसी बंद या घिरे हुए स्थान से बाहर आने का संकेत देती है, और dense forest उसी बंद स्थान को दर्शाता है। इसलिए “emerged out of the dense forest” सबसे सटीक और व्याकरणिक रूप से सही प्रयोग है। • ‘Out of’ should be used because the sentence describes the action of the hikers coming out from within a confined or enclosed place. The verb emerged naturally takes out of to indicate movement from inside the dense forest to the outside. Hence, “out of” is the most appropriate preposition here.
